lobbying_activities: 20th May 2025.  CEO invited Minister James Brown to visit St Michael's House - Letter

records.specific_details: Invitation to TD's to visit St Michael's House (December 2024)
records.intended_results: Welcoming TD's to visit St Michael's House or have Teams meeting to discuss the challenges within the disability sector and potential solutions.  It is a fantastic opportunity to work together to create positive change for individuals with disabilities and their families and carers.
lobbying_activities: Letters sent to all current TD's in December 2024.  Letter included manifesto produced by National Federation of Voluntary Service Providers. - Letter

records.specific_details: Pre-Budget Communication to the Seanad, Ministers and TD's highlighting the issues facing people with intellectual disabilities and their families, and the critical sustainability issues that are facing the organisations providing supports for these individuals - this includes St Michael's House.
records.intended_results: In Budget 2025, it is essential that the Government commits to fully resourcing and implementing the Action Plan for Disability Services 2024-2026.
lobbying_activities: Letters were sent to all Senators, TD's and Ministers. - Letter

records.intended_results: To reconsider proceeding with this outdated method, https://www.etenders.gov.ie/epps/cft/prepareViewCfTWS.do?resourceId=5530425 I propose leveraging anonymized mobile phone GPS tracking data combined with AI-driven analysis to create a more robust, scalable, and real-time solution for transport planning and management. ::Educate TDs on the near-future possibilities with AI traffic management, as mentioned during the media debate about Metrolink, highlighted by Dermot Desmond at an AI conference in Belfast https://www.irishtimes.com/ireland/dublin/2025/08/05/dublins-planned-metrolink-will-be-obsolete-because-of-artificial-intelligence-says-dermot-desmond/

The urgent sourcing of additional underwriting cover including cover for sectors unable to source affordable liability insurance. Meaningful reform and greater use of PIAB. Affordable legal fees. Ensuring insurance reform is part of govt policy. Not increasing Personal Injury Guidelines, Action Plan on Insurance Reform

records.specific_details: Seeking engagement with members of the Oireachtas to discuss the work of the Northern Ireland Assembly All-Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and discuss gambling policy on the island of Ireland
records.intended_results: To secure support from members of the Oireachtas for the work of the Northern Ireland Assembly All-Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, in particular to help draw attention to gambling marketing in sport in Ireland and the UK.
lobbying_activities: On [1 July] met with Joanna Byrne TD and Senator Chris Andrews to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Meeting
On [1 July] met with Mark Wall TD to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Email

On [17 June, 25 June] emailed Mark Wall TD seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Meeting

On [17 June, 30 June, 18 August, 20 August] emailed Emer Currie TD seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Email
On [17 June] emailed Cormac Devlin TD seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Email
On [17 June] emailed Joanna Byrne TD seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Email

On [17 June] emailed Minister James Lawless TD seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Email

On [17 June] emailed Pádraig O'Sullivan TD seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Email

On [17 June] emailed Senator Chris Andrews seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. On [3 July], sent an email with further information following our meeting on 1 July. - Email

On [17 June] emailed Senator Joe O'Reilly seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Email
On [28 August] spoke with Mark Wall TD's parliamentary assistant on the phone in relation to a 5-a-side tournament at Stormont to raise awareness of gambling advertising in sport. - Phone call

On [3 July, 10 July, 29 July, 28 August] emailed Mark Wall TD seeking further information following our meeting on 1 July, and to invite him to attend a 5-a-side tournament at Stormont to raise awareness of gambling advertising in sport. - Email

On [3 July, 5 August, 6 August] emailed Senator Chris Andrews with additional information following our meeting on 1 July, and to invite him to attend a 5-a-side tournament at Stormont to raise awareness of gambling advertising in sport. - Email

On [30 June] spoke with the office of Emer Currie TD seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Phone call
On [7 August] emailed Erin McGreehan TD seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Email

On [7 August] emailed Senator Evanne Ní Chuilinn seeking a meeting to discuss the work of the Assembly All Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and to discuss gambling marketing in sport. - Email

On [9 July, 31 July, 20 August] emailed Joanna Byrne TD to invite her to attend a 5-a-side tournament at Stormont to raise awareness of gambling advertising in sport. Asked for her assistance in putting together an Oireachtas team to participate. - Email

Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas

lobbying_activities: Letter

records.specific_details: Email concerning the E-liquids Products Tax and concerns around the mechanism chosen to implement the tax via self-declaration rather than as a tax-stamp. Hale also referred to research commissioned from KPMG which raised several important policy considerations around the E-Liquids products tax including the potential for a surge in illicit and grey market sales.
records.intended_results: A review of the proposal to apply an E-liquids tax using self-declaration mechanism rather than a tax stamp.

lobbying_activities: Discussion re proposed implementation of the E-liquids tax using a self-declaration mechanism rather than a tax stamp and highlighting the findings of an independent report from KPMG on the matter. - Meeting

Email to members of the Oireachtas referring to the proposed implementation of the E-liquids tax using a self-declaration mechanism rather than a tax stamp and highlighting the findings of an independent report from KPMG on the matter. - Email
